reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: Posted an updated version of the patch series (v2) addressing feedback from the RFC, including adding a guard for the tracepoint and renaming some functions.

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This allows for hold time estimation and provides the holder's stack at release time, which can differ from what perf lock contention captures.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer Usama Arif pointed out that the patch does not consistently check for blocked waiters, specifically suggesting to use waitqueue_active(&sem->waiters) in percpu_up_write() to match other calls.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello!

I saw that you mentioned in the commmit message that you do this for only
blocked waiters except for percpu_up_write(). We can use
waitqueue_active(&sem->waiters) to check for this over here so that
its consistent with every other call?

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This provides additional information such as hold time estimation and the holder's stack at release time, which can be useful for debugging and performance analysis. The new tracepoint has minimal impact on performance and binary size. It was previously discussed in an RFC (Request For Comments) and this patch addresses some of the feedback received.

reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin (author)
reviews.summary:
The author acknowledged a potential issue with using waitqueue_active() in the tracepoint instrumentation, agreeing that wq_has_sleeper() is more suitable since it doesn't require holding the lock and adding a barrier.

Author acknowledged a concern about the timing of the contended_release tracepoint and moved it to fire after the lock is released but before rcuwait_wake_up(), agreeing to restructure in v3
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for the feedback, Usama.

I thought about it and even mentioned in the comment, but I forgot what
was the reason. Now, I think you are correct. I added wq_has_sleeper()
locally instead of waitqueue_active() locally, since we are not holding
the lock here and waitqueue_active() requires a barrier based on the
comment. It might be not very important here, but I'd rather make it
correct even for tracepoint.

Note that __percpu_up_read() doesn't need this guard. Maybe I was
thinking at __percpu_up_read() part before and just made it symmetric.

Anyway, thanks for suggestion.

---

Good point. I moved it after this_cpu_dec() so the tracepoint fires
after the lock is released but before rcuwait_wake_up(). I also went
through all other call sites and made the placement consistent where
possible: after release, before wake. It should be fixed in v3.

2026-03-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This provides additional information such as hold time estimation and the holder's stack at release time, which can be useful for debugging and performance analysis. The new tracepoint has minimal impact on performance and binary size. It was previously discussed in an RFC (Request For Comments) and this patch addresses some of the feedback received.
